name: Foundation
description: Project scaffolding — module init, entry point, CI, README, directory structure, release configuration. All eight tasks produce the minimum skeleton needed for Phase 1 to begin.
anti_goals: No CLI API surface or cobra commands (Phase 3)
roadmap_success_criteria: go build ./... succeeds with zero errors
CI pipeline (GitHub Actions) runs on push/PR and reports green
Project directory layout established (cmd/, internal/, queries/)

tasks.title: main.go — Entry point that prints version and exits
tasks.description: Create the program entry point at cmd/deoxy/main.go. It should print a version string in the format `deoxy v0.1.0` to stdout and exit with code 0. No CLI framework yet — this is a minimal skeleton.

specification.contents:
Package main at cmd/deoxy/main.go.
- Single constant `version = "0.1.0"` (or `v0.1.0`)
- `func main()` prints `deoxy v0.1.0` to stdout, then returns (exit 0)
- No imports beyond "fmt" and "os"
- `package main` at cmd/deoxy means `deoxy` binary in root

success_criteria: cmd/deoxy/main.go exists and is valid Go
go build ./cmd/deoxy produces a working binary
./deoxy outputs 'deoxy v0.1.0' and exits with code 0
go vet ./cmd/deoxy passes with zero warnings

tasks.title: Internal structure — Ensure all subdirectories exist
tasks.description: The standard project layout must exist before any code is written. Most directories already exist (cmd/, internal/, internal/config/, internal/parser/, internal/generator/, internal/lang/). Verify they exist and create the missing `queries/` directory (for tree-sitter .scm query files) and `queries/go/`, `queries/python/`, `queries/c/`, `queries/cpp/`, `queries/rust/` subdirectories. Also ensure internal subdirectories have Go package stub files so `go test ./...` can work on empty packages.

specification.stubs: Create empty Go stub files (e.g., internal/config/doc.go with `package config`) for each internal package so the compiler doesn't complain about empty directories. Each stub should have a descriptive package comment.

success_criteria: All internal/ subpackages exist with Go stub files (at minimum a doc.go or similar)
queries/ and its per-language subdirectories exist
go build ./internal/... succeeds
Directory tree matches standard Go project layout

tasks.title: Placeholder test — so go test ./... passes
tasks.description: Create a trivial passing test so that `go test ./...` returns zero failures. Ideally place it at the root package or in an internal package. Should be a no-op test that simply asserts true — its purpose is to make CI pass until real tests are written in later phases.

specification.package: Choose an internal package that gets imported by future code or just root. Prefer a dedicated package like `internal/version/` or simply add a test to an existing internal package.
specification.rationale: go test ./... fails if no test files exist at all in a valid package — an empty package with no _test.go is fine, but if the root package has no .go files it won't compile. The test ensures the CI gate passes.

success_criteria: Test file compiles without errors
go test ./... passes with at least 1 test run (not 0 tests)
Test can be removed in Phase 1 once real tests exist

tasks.title: Makefile — build, test, lint, clean targets
tasks.description:
A standard Go project Makefile with the following targets:
- `build`: builds the deoxy binary at ./deoxy (or ./bin/deoxy)
- `test`: runs `go test ./... -v -count=1`
- `lint`: runs `golangci-lint run ./...` (if available, else `go vet ./...`)
- `clean`: removes the built binary and any artifacts
- `all` (default): build + test + lint

Should use `$(BINARY)` and `$(VERSION)` variables for easy modification. Do not add any Phase-specific logic (e.g., embedded grammars).

specification.notes: PHONY declarations for all targets. Use the -ldflags approach to inject version at build time (links with P0-T1's version variable).

success_criteria: Makefile exists at project root
make build produces a working binary at ./deoxy
make test passes
make clean removes the binary
make lint runs go vet (golangci-lint optional, no failure if missing)

tasks.title: README.md — Project overview, install instructions, development guide
tasks.description:
A comprehensive README covering:
- Project name and tagline ("Multi-language documentation comment generator")
- Badges (build status placeholder, Go version, license)
- Features overview (bullet list of what deoxy does — parsing, multi-language, templates, CLI + VS Code)
- Quick start / installation (go install, download from releases, build from source)
- Usage examples (stub — real CLI output once Phase 3 ships)
- Development guide (prerequisites, building, testing, project structure)
- Supported languages
- Configuration (reference to .deoxy.yaml)
- License section

Keep placeholder sections for features that don't exist yet but mark them clearly.

sections: Features Overview (6-8 bullet points showing all planned capabilities)
Quick Start / Usage (stub — will be filled in Phase 3)
Development (prerequisites, `make build`, `make test`, project layout tree)

success_criteria: README.md exists at project root
README.md is valid markdown (no broken syntax)
Covers all required sections
Contains the actual `go install` command with the module path
Badge placeholders use shields.io or similar (or plain-text stubs)

tasks.title: LICENSE — MIT license file
tasks.description: Standard MIT license with the project holder's name. Use the year 2026. The copyright holder should match whatever the user specifies (default: 'Piyush Harne' based on the module name 'piyuxh').

specification.rationale: MIT is the standard permissive license for Go CLI tools, consistent with the project's open-source nature.

success_criteria: LICENSE file exists
Contains the MIT license text verbatim
Copyright year is 2026
Copyright holder name is correct

tasks.title: CI workflow — GitHub Actions: lint, build, test on ubuntu/macos/windows
tasks.description:
A GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml that:
- Triggers on push to main/master and on all pull requests
- Builds matrix: OS (ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest), Go version 1.26 (latest stable, matching go.mod's 1.26.3)
- Steps: checkout, setup Go cache, run go vet, run go build ./..., run go test ./...
- Does NOT install golangci-lint (keep it simple — go vet is sufficient for Phase 0)
- Does NOT run any release steps (separate goreleaser workflow)
- Does NOT cache Go build cache beyond the default setup-go caching
- Does NOT depend on Makefile (use raw go commands for reliability on all platforms)

Use the actions/setup-go@v5 action with go-version-file matching go.mod.
success_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists
Workflow triggers on push and pull_request
Runs on all three OS targets (ubuntu, macOS, windows)
Go version matches 1.26.x from go.mod
Steps execute in order: vet → build → test
Valid YAML (no syntax errors)

tasks.title: .goreleaser.yaml — GoReleaser config for cross-compilation
tasks.description:
GoReleaser configuration file at project root for cross-compiling the deoxy binary to all target platforms. Must support:
- OS: linux, darwin (macOS), windows
- Arch: amd64, arm64
- Use the standard GoReleaser defaults
- Build from ./cmd/deoxy/main.go
- Binary name: deoxy (deoxy.exe on Windows)
- No release automation (no GitHub release config — that's added when releasing)
- No extra files/archives beyond the binary
- Version injected via -ldflags with -X main.version={{.Version}}
- Use GoReleaser v2 config format (current as of 2026)

This config is the foundation that a future release workflow (Phase 4 or later) will use.
success_criteria: .goreleaser.yaml exists at project root
Valid YAML (no syntax errors)
Cross-compiles to all 6 target combinations (linux + darwin + windows × amd64 + arm64)
CGO_ENABLED=0 set for all targets
Version injection via ldflags matches main.go's version variable

execution_order.rationale: No dependencies — main.go, directory stubs, and LICENSE can all be created in parallel
P0-T3 needs directory structure (P0-T2), P0-T5 needs main.go (P0-T1) for install cmd, P0-T8 needs main.go for build config
Makefile needs both main.go and tests; CI needs build + test to verify in CI

verification.expected: exit code 0, at least 1 test run
